chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C. P. NO. D-580 / 2019 a/w C. P. NO. D-1182, 1476, 1477, 1743, 2421, 5785 7266, 7267 / 2019 C. P. NO. D-2316, 2317, 2506, 3659, 4439, 4440, 5252, 5253, 5262 / 2020 C. P. NO. D-7126, 7496, 7497 & 7498 of 2021 C. P. NO. D-505, 506, 1049, 1050, 1544, 2463, 3065, 3066, 3067 4191 4537, 4752, 5194, 5195, 5196, 5197, 5263, 5264, 5265, 5490, 6895, 6896, 6897, 7061, 7062, 7560 / 2022 C. P. NO. D-74 / 2023 ___________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge ___________________________________________________________ D/O MATTER For hearing of CMA No. 5970/2023. 09.03.2023. Ms. Khalid Javed Khan, Owais Ali Shah & Jam Zeeshan, Advocates for Petitioners. Mr. Ameer Bakhsh Metlo, Advocate for Respondent. Mr. Rana Sakhawat Ali, Advocate for Respondent. _____________ These applications have been filed in all listed Petitions seeking suspension of Judgment / Order dated 28.02.2023 whereby, these Petitions were dismissed. All Learned Counsel jointly submit that the Petitioners intend to challenge the judgment before Supreme Court; hence the department be restrained from enforcing immediate recovery, as the petitioners would be exposed to heavy financial burden causing serious prejudice to them. We have heard all learned Counsel and have gone through the contents of the applications and supporting affidavits filed along with these applications and are of the view that since the petitions stands dismissed after hearing the parties, whereas, such petitions were pending since long with ad-interim orders in favor of the Petitioners; at this stage of the proceedings, no prima facie case is made out to suspend the judgment in question.

All Learned Counsel jointly submit that the Petitioners intend to challenge the judgment before Supreme Court; hence the department be restrained from enforcing immediate recovery, as the petitioners would be exposed to heavy financial burden causing serious prejudice to them. We have heard all learned Counsel and have gone through the contents of the applications and supporting affidavits filed along with these applications and are of the view that since the petitions stands dismissed after hearing the parties, whereas, such petitions were pending since long with ad-interim orders in favor of the Petitioners; at this stage of the proceedings, no prima facie case is made out to suspend the judgment in question. All these applications do not merit any consideration and therefore, are hereby dismissed. Office to place copy of this order in above connected Petitions. J U D G E J U D G E Arshad/
